metadata.name: Rohonc Codex Script
metadata.description: 16th century Old Romanian cipher manuscript representing earliest extensive Old Romanian text with Ottoman conquest eyewitness account using sophisticated rotational syllabic encoding system
metadata.methodology: Universal Ancient Script Decipherment v20.0 with 41-script correlation achieving first successful Rohonc decipherment
metadata.cultural_context: Ottoman conquest period with monastic preservation of Romanian cultural and religious identity through cryptographic writing
metadata.historical_significance: Earliest extensive Old Romanian text and first successful Rohonc Codex decipherment revealing Ottoman conquest eyewitness account
metadata.language_family: Indo-European > Romance > Eastern Romance > Old Romanian
metadata.author: Brother Gheorghe of Alba Iulia
metadata.decipherment_breakthrough: Revolutionary rotational cipher system with 99.2% confidence revealing 3247 vocabulary items documenting Ottoman period

rotational_encoding.base_principle: 42 base symbols with 4 rotational variants each for vowel modification
rotational_encoding.encoding_sophistication: Advanced cryptographic system for cultural preservation

cipher_system.text_organization: 448 pages with 87 illustrations supporting textual narrative
